headline: Parking Policy Reform Stalls in 8 Major Malaysian Cities
context: Proposed parking reform to introduce dynamic pricing shelved by 8 of 12 targeted city councils since 2023.

cards.big: 8 of 12 city councils shelved parking reform proposals since 2023
cards.sub: Political resistance to reducing free parking keeps cities car-dependent

cards.big: KL has 4.2 million parking spaces for 1.8 million resident population
cards.sub: Parking occupies 28% of city centre land versus 12% in comparable Asian capitals

cards.big: Mandatory minimums add RM18,000-35,000 to the cost of each housing unit
cards.sub: Developers pass parking structure cost directly to property buyers

cards.big: Penang implemented dynamic pricing in 2024 reducing congestion by 18%
cards.sub: Revenue of RM42 million funded 12km cycling lanes and pedestrian walkways

cards.big: Parking policy tests whether cities prioritize car storage or people movement
Parking reform stalling in 8 cities shows how politically difficult it is to price road space correctly.
